items.content_statement: I have the confidence and motivation to move in different ways
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - confidence and motivation are dispositional qualities that exist only as enacted over time across contexts

items.content_statement: I am beginning to develop control of gross motor movements and fine motor movements in different environments, moving safely in response to instructions
items.classification_rationale: Hierarchical procedural skill - motor control develops in prerequisite sequence with observable criteria for proficiency levels

items.content_statement: I am beginning to make connections between my diet and my physical health and well-being.
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about causal relationships between diet and health outcomes that must be understood - a conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation or application tasks.

items.content_statement: I am beginning to recognise the connection between the physical and emotional changes that can occur in different contexts
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about how physical and emotional changes are connected - a conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation or application tasks

items.content_statement: I am beginning to recognise some of the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about what affects physical health - factual content that can be assessed through knowledge checks

items.content_statement: I am beginning to know how to respond and get help when behaviours, conditions and situations affect my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Procedural knowledge - specific steps/procedures for responding and getting help that can be assessed through demonstration

items.content_statement: I can use and improve basic movement skills in familiar and unfamiliar situations.
items.classification_rationale: Procedural skill with clear performance criteria - Type 1

items.content_statement: I can respond to prompts in imaginative and creative ways.
items.classification_rationale: Creative response capability deployed when situations call for it - Type 2

items.content_statement: I have the confidence and motivation to persevere when faced with physical challenges.
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation that operates by default across contexts - Type 3

items.content_statement: I have developed an understanding that I need a balanced diet
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about nutritional requirements - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation

items.content_statement: I can make informed choices about the food I eat and prepare to support my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when food choices arise - reasoning quality in applying nutritional understanding to specific decisions
prerequisite_lts: I have developed an understanding that I need a balanced diet

items.content_statement: I can describe the way in which physical and emotional changes are connected in different contexts.
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about connections between physical and emotional changes that must be understood - a conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation tasks.

items.content_statement: I can recognise some of the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Recognition capability - propositional knowledge about what affects physical health and well-being that can be assessed through knowledge test

items.content_statement: I know how to respond and get help in a safe way when behaviours, conditions and situations affect my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Procedural knowledge - specific steps/procedures for responding and getting help that can be assessed through demonstration or rubric
prerequisite_lts: I can recognise some of the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being

items.content_statement: I can develop and apply a range of skills in familiar, unfamiliar and changing situations, exploring space creatively in response to a variety of stimuli.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - adapting skills across different contexts and responding creatively to varied stimuli

items.content_statement: I can motivate myself to engage confidently in regular physical activity and sport.
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - self-motivation for regular engagement operates across contexts over time

items.content_statement: I am aware of my own progress.
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - ongoing self-awareness of progress operates as a maintained capacity across contexts

items.content_statement: I can explain the importance of a balanced diet and nutrition and the impact my choices have on my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships (diet affects health) that can be explained and assessed through clear criteria

items.content_statement: I can plan and prepare basic, nutritious meals
items.classification_rationale: Procedural skill with clear performance criteria that can be demonstrated and assessed
prerequisite_lts: I can explain the importance of a balanced diet and nutrition and the impact my choices have on my physical health and well-being

items.content_statement: I can explain the way in which physical and emotional changes are connected in different contexts
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal connections that must be understood

items.content_statement: I can monitor, review and adapt my behaviour to support my physical and emotional health, setting myself relevant targets
items.classification_rationale: sustained orientation of self-regulation enacted over time across contexts
prerequisite_lts: I can explain the way in which physical and emotional changes are connected in different contexts

items.content_statement: I can describe the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about health factors - factual content that can be assessed through knowledge test

items.content_statement: I know how to respond to and/or manage behaviours, conditions and situations in order to actively reduce the risk of harm to myself
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when health-risk situations arise - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning about appropriate responses
prerequisite_lts: I can describe the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being

items.content_statement: I can transfer a range of movement skills from familiar to unfamiliar and changing situations and environments, using space creatively in response to a variety of stimuli.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - transfer and creative adaptation across multiple contexts with no single correct answer

items.content_statement: I can engage in regular physical activity and sport with confidence, motivation and commitment.
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - confidence, motivation and commitment in physical activity exists only as enacted over time across contexts

items.content_statement: knowledge and understanding of a balanced diet and nutrition
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about nutrition concepts that must be understood before application

items.content_statement: apply knowledge and understanding of a balanced diet and nutrition to make choices which will allow me to maintain my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when making food choices - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning quality assessment
prerequisite_lts: knowledge and understanding of a balanced diet and nutrition

items.content_statement: plan and prepare a variety of nutritious meals
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear performance criteria that can be assessed through demonstration
prerequisite_lts: knowledge and understanding of a balanced diet and nutrition

items.content_statement: I can analyse the connection between physical and emotional changes
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for examining physical-emotional connections - Type 2 reasoning skill

items.content_statement: I can modify my behaviour to support my physical and emotional health
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered capability to adapt behaviour based on health analysis - Type 2 skill deployed when health situations arise

items.content_statement: I can work collaboratively to plan and refine strengths and areas for improvements
items.classification_rationale: Collaborative planning and refinement capability deployed in specific improvement contexts - Type 2 analytical skill

items.content_statement: I can explain the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - understanding how specific factors affect health outcomes

items.content_statement: through my actions, I can respond to and/or manage behaviours, conditions and situations in order to actively reduce the risk of harm to myself and to others
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical capability - deploying response strategies when health-affecting situations arise
prerequisite_lts: I can explain the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being

items.content_statement: I can independently adapt and apply movement skills across a range of activities and environments, managing space creatively in response to a variety of stimuli.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - adapting movement skills requires reasoning about context, space, and stimuli with no single correct response

items.content_statement: I can value the benefits of participation in regular physical activity and sport, seeking opportunities to develop my expertise.
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - valuing benefits and seeking opportunities describes an ongoing enacted capacity that operates across contexts over time

items.content_statement: knowledge and understanding of a balanced diet and nutrition
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about nutrition principles that must be understood before application

items.content_statement: I can adjust my diet in response to different contexts and apply my knowledge and understanding of a balanced diet and nutrition to support others
items.classification_rationale: analytical capability deployed when situations call for dietary adjustment or supporting others - occasion-triggered skill
prerequisite_lts: knowledge and understanding of a balanced diet and nutrition

items.content_statement: I can apply a range of techniques to prepare a variety of nutritious meals
items.classification_rationale: procedural skill with clear technique sequences that can be demonstrated and assessed
prerequisite_lts: knowledge and understanding of a balanced diet and nutrition

items.content_statement: I can evaluate the connection between physical and emotional changes, independently selecting from a range of strategies to improve my physical and emotional health and that of others.
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - evaluating connections and selecting strategies. Multiple valid approaches exist for connecting physical and emotional changes, and reasoning quality determines assessment outcome.

items.content_statement: I can apply my knowledge of the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships (what affects physical health) that must be understood before application

items.content_statement: I can apply knowledge to keep myself and others safe
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when safety situations arise, requiring reasoning about context and appropriate responses
prerequisite_lts: I can apply my knowledge of the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being

items.content_statement: I can safely intervene, using learnt techniques, when others' physical health is at risk
items.classification_rationale: Procedural skill with specific techniques that can be demonstrated and assessed against clear performance criteria
prerequisite_lts: I can apply my knowledge of the behaviours, conditions and situations that affect my physical health and well-being

items.content_statement: I have an awareness of my perceptions and thoughts. I can focus my attention and am aware of being able to do this. I am beginning to have an awareness that thoughts and feelings change, and I am starting to notice when change happens.
items.classification_rationale: This describes sustained orientations that operate by default across contexts - ongoing awareness of one's mental processes, sustained attention capacity, and developing metacognitive awareness of emotional change. These are enacted capacities that exist only across time and contexts.

items.content_statement: I can notice and communicate how I am feeling
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical capability - noticing and communicating feelings when situations call for it

items.content_statement: I am beginning to have an awareness of how feelings are communicated through actions
items.classification_rationale: Propositional understanding about how feelings and actions connect - conceptual knowledge to be understood

items.content_statement: I have an awareness of the feelings of others. I am aware of when others are kind to me and when I am kind to others.
items.classification_rationale: Step 3: This describes a sustained default orientation - ongoing awareness of others' feelings and kindness patterns that exists as an enacted capacity across contexts over time

items.content_statement: I can, with support, focus attention on my perceptions and thoughts
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for self-reflection - occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: I can understand how and why my thoughts, feelings and actions change in response to different experiences
items.classification_rationale: Propositional understanding of causal relationships between experiences and internal responses

items.content_statement: I can notice and communicate my feelings
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for emotional awareness and expression - occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: I am beginning to notice when I need help to manage my feelings
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation of self-awareness and help-seeking that operates by default across contexts - dispositional knowledge

items.content_statement: I can reflect on my experiences
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when occasions call for reflection - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: I can pay attention to the feelings of others and I am learning to think about why they may feel that way.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained orientation of attending to others' feelings and considering their perspectives - a capacity that exists only as enacted over time across contexts, not deployed on specific occasions

items.content_statement: I can recognise the benefits of being able to focus attention on my perceptions and thoughts and know that I am developing my self-awareness
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the benefits of focused attention and self-awareness development - factual understanding that can be assessed through knowledge check

items.content_statement: I can self-regulate my emotions in a healthy way using strategies that I have developed
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation - emotional self-regulation operates as a default capacity across contexts and exists only in enactment over time

items.content_statement: I can see the benefits of communicating about feelings as one of a range of strategies which can help promote positive mental health and emotional well-being
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about causal relationships between communication strategies and mental health outcomes

items.content_statement: I can ask for help when I need it from people I trust
items.classification_rationale: sustained orientation of help-seeking that operates by default across contexts when support is needed

items.content_statement: I can reflect on the way that past events and experiences have affected my thoughts, feelings and actions. I can anticipate how future events may make me and others feel.
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability - reflecting on cause-effect relationships between experiences and responses, and anticipating emotional impacts. These are occasion-triggered skills deployed when situations call for reflection or anticipation, not sustained default orientations.

items.content_statement: I can empathise with others
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - empathy operates as an ongoing enacted capacity across contexts, not deployed only when situations call for it

items.content_statement: I can understand how and why experiences affect me and others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about causal relationships - understanding how experiences affect people is conceptual content to be grasped

items.content_statement: I can independently focus attention on my perceptions, thoughts and feelings in order to further develop my self-awareness
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for self-reflection - occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: I can identify different strategies to self-regulate my emotions in response to a range of experiences
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when emotional regulation is needed - occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: I can advocate the benefits of communicating about feelings as one of a range of strategies which can help promote positive mental health and emotional well-being
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when advocacy situations arise - requires reasoning about benefits and strategies across multiple contexts

items.content_statement: I can identify people and groups who can help me with my mental health and emotional well-being
items.classification_rationale: Procedural skill with clear criteria - identifying specific resources and support networks

items.content_statement: I can reflect and learn from the past in order to anticipate and prepare myself and others for future experiences.
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when situations call for reflection and preparation - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning quality assessment

items.content_statement: I can understand the value of demonstrating empathy through actions which are compassionate and kind
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the value/importance of empathetic actions - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation or reasoning tasks

items.content_statement: I can empathise with others and demonstrate this through actions which are compassionate and kind
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation of empathy enacted through compassionate and kind actions across contexts - exists only as enacted over time
prerequisite_lts: I can understand the value of demonstrating empathy through actions which are compassionate and kind

items.content_statement: I can use my self-awareness to appreciate the complexity of my emotions
items.classification_rationale: propositional knowledge about emotional complexity that must be understood

items.content_statement: I can apply strategies to self-regulate emotions in a healthy way when situations call for it
items.classification_rationale: occasion-triggered analytical skill deployed when emotional regulation is needed
prerequisite_lts: I can use my self-awareness to appreciate the complexity of my emotions

items.content_statement: I can self-regulate emotions in a healthy way and connect with others as a sustained orientation
items.classification_rationale: sustained default orientation of healthy emotional regulation and connection enacted across contexts over time
prerequisite_lts: I can use my self-awareness to appreciate the complexity of my emotions
I can apply strategies to self-regulate emotions in a healthy way when situations call for it

items.content_statement: I can contribute towards a culture where talking about mental health and emotional well-being is encouraged and normalised.
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - contributing to culture operates by default across contexts, not just when prompted

items.content_statement: I can identify when to seek help based on a good understanding of my mental health and emotional well-being.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about mental health and emotional well-being that enables identification of help-seeking moments

items.content_statement: I can respond to current experiences, as well as reflect, and learn from the past, in order to anticipate and prepare myself and others for future experiences.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - involves reasoning across multiple temporal lenses (past, present, future) and applying learning to prepare for experiences

items.content_statement: I can empathise with others which helps me to be compassionate and kind towards myself and others.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained orientation that exists only as enacted over time - empathy, compassion and kindness are dispositional qualities that operate by default across contexts rather than being deployed on specific occasions.

items.content_statement: I can make decisions based on what I like and dislike
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when decision situations arise - reasoning about preferences to guide choices. Multiple valid approaches exist for preference-based decision-making, with quality determined by reasoning coherence rather than single correct answers.

items.content_statement: I have developed an awareness that my decisions can affect me and others
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation - an ongoing awareness that operates across contexts, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: I can take part in group decisions
items.classification_rationale: Describes an occasion-triggered capability deployed when group decision situations arise, involving reasoning and participation skills

items.content_statement: I have an understanding that things can be safe or unsafe.
items.classification_rationale: This is propositional knowledge about a conceptual distinction (safe vs unsafe) that learners must understand - a foundational concept that builds understanding of safety categories.

items.content_statement: I can make decisions based on what I know.
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when decision situations arise - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: I can recognise that my decisions can impact on me and others, both now and in the future.
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about cause-and-effect relationships between decisions and impacts - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation or application

items.content_statement: I can take part in group decisions.
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered capability deployed when group decision situations arise - analytical/collaborative skill that varies in quality of reasoning and execution

items.content_statement: I understand why some decisions need to be made as a group.
items.classification_rationale: Conceptual understanding of principles governing when group decision-making is appropriate - propositional knowledge about decision-making contexts

items.content_statement: I can identify and assess risks
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for risk evaluation - multiple valid frameworks exist for risk assessment, quality of reasoning determines outcome

items.content_statement: I can make considered decisions, taking into account available information, including past experiences
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when decision situations arise - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: I can set appropriate goals
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when goal-setting situations arise - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: Some decisions I make will have a long-term impact on my life and the lives of others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how decisions work - a causal claim about decision consequences that learners must understand

items.content_statement: Decisions can be made individually and collectively, and that they can be influenced by a range of factors
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about the nature of decision-making processes - factual content about how decisions work

items.content_statement: I can recognise that some decisions I make will have a long-term impact on my life and the lives of others
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when evaluating specific decisions - occasion-triggered skill for assessing decision impact
prerequisite_lts: Some decisions I make will have a long-term impact on my life and the lives of others

items.content_statement: I can identify and assess risks, and I can take steps to reduce them.
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when situations call for risk evaluation - multiple valid approaches exist for identifying and assessing risks, with reasoning quality determining assessment outcome

items.content_statement: I can research, examine and evaluate a range of evidence to make considered and informed decisions.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when decision-making situations arise; quality of reasoning determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: I can set appropriate goals and plan a course of action to achieve them.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability for goal-setting and planning deployed when situations call for it; quality of reasoning and appropriateness determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: I can consider relevant factors and implications when making decisions individually and collectively.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when decision-making situations arise - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: I can anticipate, assess and manage risks.
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when situations call for risk evaluation - multiple valid approaches exist, quality of reasoning determines outcome

items.content_statement: I can set appropriate goals, plan a course of action and overcome challenges to achieve them.
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when goal-setting situations arise - involves reasoning about appropriateness, planning strategies, and problem-solving approaches across contexts

items.content_statement: I can critically evaluate factors and implications, including risks, when making decisions individually and collectively.
items.classification_rationale: Describes analytical capability deployed when decision-making situations arise - occasion-triggered skill with reasoning quality as the assessment focus

items.content_statement: I can recognise and follow rules and norms in the groups and situations in which I take part.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - recognising context-specific rules and norms requires reasoning across multiple social frameworks

items.content_statement: I can show care and respect for others
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation that exists only as enacted across contexts over time - showing care and respect is a dispositional quality that operates by default rather than being deployed when prompted

items.content_statement: I can recognise and follow the rules and norms of different groups and situations in which I take part.
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - recognising context-specific rules and norms across different groups requires situational analysis and appropriate response selection

items.content_statement: I can change how I interact and behave in different situations with support.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained capacity for behavioural flexibility that exists only as enacted across different contexts over time - a dispositional orientation rather than an occasion-triggered skill.

items.content_statement: I can recognise that there are similarities and differences between people's values and attitudes.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when encountering people or situations - recognising patterns in values and attitudes across different contexts. This is occasion-prompted reasoning skill, not sustained orientation.

items.content_statement: I have an understanding of the rules, norms and behaviours of different groups and situations, and I recognise that these have an influence on me.
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about how social influences work - factual/conceptual content about rules, norms, behaviours and their effects that the learner must understand.

items.content_statement: I can interact pro-socially in different groups and situations.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained orientation that operates by default across contexts - pro-social interaction as an enacted capacity that exists only as it is demonstrated over time and across different groups and situations.

items.content_statement: I have developed an understanding that my values, attitudes and identity are shaped by different groups and influences.
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about how identity formation works - a conceptual understanding of causal relationships that can be explained and demonstrated through examples.

items.content_statement: I have a developing awareness of how rules, norms and behaviours become established within groups and at times go unchallenged
items.classification_rationale: Decision tree step 3: describes a sustained default orientation ('developing awareness') that exists only as enacted over time across contexts

items.content_statement: I can interact pro-socially in different groups and situations, adapting my behaviours accordingly.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained orientation that operates by default across contexts - the capacity to consistently interact pro-socially and adapt behaviours appropriately exists only as it is enacted over time and across different social situations.

items.content_statement: I can evaluate how my values, attitudes and identity are shaped by the groups and social influences with which I interact.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to examine social influences - requires reasoning across multiple frameworks about identity formation, no single correct answer but better/worse analyses

items.content_statement: I have an understanding of the complexities of groups and situations, the interactions that take place within and between them, and their effect on those exposed to them.
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about how groups function and their effects - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation of the concepts and their relationships

items.content_statement: I can interact pro-socially in different groups and situations, and actively advocate for other individuals and groups.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained orientation that operates by default across contexts - pro-social interaction and advocacy as an enacted capacity that exists only through sustained practice over time and situations.

items.content_statement: I can recognise and understand how people's values, attitudes and identity are shaped by different groups and influences.
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical capability deployed when examining how social influences shape identity - multiple valid interpretive frameworks exist for understanding these dynamics, and reasoning quality determines assessment outcome.

items.content_statement: I can promote positive attitudes and values, and, where appropriate, I can challenge harmful ones.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained default orientation - the capacity to consistently promote positive attitudes/values and challenge harmful ones operates as an ongoing enacted quality across contexts, not just when prompted by specific situations.

items.content_statement: I can identify who looks after me and who my family and friends are.
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about specific people in the learner's life - factual content that can be directly assessed through identification tasks.

items.content_statement: I can communicate my needs and feelings in my relationships.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained orientation that operates by default across relationship contexts - the capacity to communicate needs and feelings exists only as it is enacted over time and across different relationships.

items.content_statement: I can get along with others with and without support
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained social orientation that exists only as enacted across contexts and relationships over time, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: I can recognise that there are different types of relationships beyond my family and friends.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when encountering relationship contexts - recognising and categorising relationship types requires reasoning across multiple frameworks

items.content_statement: I can communicate my needs and feelings in my relationships, and notice the needs and feelings of others.
items.classification_rationale: This describes analytical/reasoning capability deployed when relationship situations call for it - communicating needs/feelings and noticing others' needs/feelings are occasion-triggered skills that require judgment about how to respond appropriately in specific interpersonal contexts.

items.content_statement: I can make friends and try to resolve disagreements, seeking support when needed.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical or reasoning capability deployed when social situations call for it - making friends and resolving disagreements requires contextual judgment and multiple valid approaches

items.content_statement: There are differences within types of relationships and that relationships change over time
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how relationships work - factual/conceptual claims about relationship dynamics that learners must understand

items.content_statement: I can communicate my needs and feelings, and respond to those of others
items.classification_rationale: Analytical/reasoning capability deployed when communication situations arise - occasion-triggered skill requiring judgment about how to communicate effectively in different contexts

items.content_statement: I can identify conflict in relationships and take steps to resolve it
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical skill - deployed when conflict situations arise, involves reasoning about interpersonal dynamics and selecting appropriate resolution strategies

items.content_statement: I can make and maintain relationships that matter to me
items.classification_rationale: Sustained orientation - relationship-making and maintenance operates as a default capacity across contexts over time, not just when prompted

items.content_statement: I can reflect on the characteristics of safe relationships
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when prompted to examine relationships - reasoning quality determines assessment outcome

items.content_statement: I can seek support when needed
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered capability deployed when situations call for it - involves judgment about when support is needed

items.content_statement: I can respect the rights of others
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - respecting others' rights operates as an ongoing enacted capacity across contexts, not deployed only when prompted
prerequisite_lts: I understand how these rights impact on myself and others

items.content_statement: I understand how these rights impact on myself and others
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge - understanding causal relationships and consequences of rights

items.content_statement: I can show a developing awareness of the complex nature of relationships
items.classification_rationale: Describes a sustained default orientation - 'developing awareness' that exists as an ongoing state across contexts, not an occasion-triggered skill

items.content_statement: I can communicate my needs and feelings, and respect those of others.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained orientation that operates by default across contexts - the capacity to communicate needs/feelings and respect others exists only as it is enacted over time in real relationships

items.content_statement: I can form and maintain healthy relationships with a wider circle of people
items.classification_rationale: Sustained default orientation - forming and maintaining relationships is an ongoing capacity enacted across contexts over time

items.content_statement: I can respect other people's points of view and use this to help resolve conflict
items.classification_rationale: Occasion-triggered analytical skill - deployed when conflict situations arise, involving multiple perspectives and reasoning quality

items.content_statement: I can consider the role of safety in relationships
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge about how safety functions in relationships - conceptual understanding that can be assessed through explanation

items.content_statement: I can identify where my safety or the safety of others is threatened
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for threat assessment - occasion-triggered skill requiring reasoning quality

items.content_statement: I know how to respond when safety is threatened
items.classification_rationale: Propositional knowledge of response procedures - factual content that can be assessed through knowledge check

items.content_statement: I can exercise my own rights and respect those of others, and I can recognise that rights can be infringed.
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - recognising rights infringement and balancing competing rights claims requires reasoning across multiple frameworks

items.content_statement: I can show an understanding of the complex nature of relationships in a range of contexts and an understanding of how they are influenced by a range of factors.
items.classification_rationale: This describes propositional knowledge about relationships - conceptual understanding of their complex nature and influencing factors that can be assessed through explanation or application tasks.

items.content_statement: I can make meaningful connections with others, valuing safe, healthy and equitable relationships in a range of contexts.
items.classification_rationale: This describes a sustained default orientation - the capacity to make meaningful connections and value healthy relationships operates as an ongoing disposition across contexts, not as an occasion-triggered skill deployed when prompted.

items.content_statement: I can take steps to avoid conflict and to remove myself from unsafe relationships
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - assessing relationship dynamics and choosing appropriate responses

items.content_statement: I can draw on support systems for myself and others when needed
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for it - recognizing when support is needed and identifying appropriate resources

items.content_statement: I can advocate the rights of myself and others
items.classification_rationale: Analytical capability deployed when situations call for advocacy - requires reasoning about rights, context, and appropriate advocacy strategies
